advantages: ColumnTransformer plus Pipeline keeps per-column encoding choices versioned and reproducible from training through production serving.
Cardinality-aware strategy selection avoids memory blow-ups from blind one-hot expansion on high-level ID columns.
Rare-level bucketing and frequency encoding compress long tails while preserving signal for tree and regularized linear models.
Explicit links to Encoding Categoricals separate workflow decisions from canonical one-hot, ordinal, and target definitions—reducing duplicated documentation.
disadvantages: Multiple encoding strategies per dataset increase pipeline complexity and debugging surface (feature names, branch order, unseen-category behavior).
Heuristic cardinality cutoffs (e.g. 50 vs 500 levels) fail without dataset-specific cross-validation—no universal default exists.
Hash and frequency encodings sacrifice per-level interpretability compared to one-hot coefficients or clear ordinal steps.
Native categorical support varies by library—workflows that mix sklearn and specialized boosters need parallel encoding paths or export conversions.
